name: Like Like Drive Inn
tagline: A 24-hour diner serving old-school Hawaiian comfort food since 1953 — oxtail soup, pancakes, and loco moco at 3am.
talkStory: Pronounced 'lee-keh lee-keh' (named after nearby Likelike Highway, which honors Princess Miriam Likelike, sister of King Kalakaua), this 24-hour diner has been feeding Honolulu since 1953. The oxtail soup with ginger and star anise is the signature — locals debate whether it rivals Asahi Grill's version. The pancakes are old-school fluffy, and the loco moco hits different at 3am. After-bar crowds, night-shift workers, and early-morning fishermen all converge in vinyl booths and Formica tables that haven't changed in decades.
